title: Incremental loads and watermarks
description: Incremental loads move only new/changed rows via a watermark or CDC; full reloads are simple but do not scale and risk overwriting good data.
body: Use when deciding how much data to move each run. Incremental extraction tracks a high-watermark (max updated_at) or uses change-data-capture to pull only deltas, which scales but must handle late-arriving and updated rows. Full reloads are simple and self-correcting but expensive and, if a source read fails midway, can replace good data with partial data. Do not full-reload large sources routinely. Actions: use a persisted watermark or CDC with idempotent merge. Validation signals include a stable watermark advancing and counts matching expected deltas. Risk boundary: a watermark that misses updates silently drops changes; verify late/updated-row handling.
